publishedAbstract: In some instances, it may be desirable to change the storageclass of a pv-pool backingstore, generally after a migration from Standalone MCG, to Full Internal ODF Deployment, which this solution will cover. **NOTE:** This solution will cover a generic source pv-pool, to a destination pv-pool backingstore migration. However, in the event the PV-pool is the `noobaa-default-backing-store`, review the respective Troubleshooting Product Documentation that correlates with the version of ODF, as the backing store may need to be overridden. For example: [3.2. Overriding the default backing store ODF v4.21](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_openshift_data_foundation/4.14/html/managing_hybrid_and_multicloud_resources/adding-storage-resources-for-hybrid-or-multicloud_rhodf#overriding-the-default-backing-store_rhodf) **Related Articles:** [Migrating From Standalone Multicloud Gateway (MCG/NooBaa) to ODF/Ceph Full Deployment (Deployed Using StorageCluster)- OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7055285) [How to Migrate the db-noobaa-db-pg- StorageClass to Ceph RBD after Migrating from Standalone Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG) to Full Openshift Data Foundation (ODF) Internal Deployment v4.18 and Below](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7140944) [How to Migrate the db-noobaa-db-pg- StorageClass to Ceph RBD after Migrating from Standalone Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG) to Full Openshift Data Foundation (ODF) Internal Deployment v4.19+](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7140980)
publishedTitle: Migrating the PV-Pool Backingstore Storageclass to Ceph RBD after Standalone Multicloud Gateway (MCG/NooBaa) to Full OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF) Internal Deployment
